title: Global leaders hail Modi as he surpasses Nehru's record as longest-serving elected PM
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has set a new record as India's longest-serving elected Prime Minister. He has now served more consecutive days in office than Jawaharlal Nehru. Leaders from the United States, Malaysia, and Sri Lanka have sent congratulations. They praised his leadership and public service. The US ambassador highlighted his decades of dedication.

title: 'Told him his son was in Jaipur': Man dies in ICU unaware Delhi hotel fire killed 8 family members
content: A 76-year-old man, Radheshyam Aggarwal, has passed away after losing eight relatives in a devastating Hauz Rani fire. His family had stayed at the B&B to be near him in the hospital, unaware of the tragedy. He died without knowing his wife, son, daughter-in-law, and granddaughters perished.

title: Huge blow to India! Hardik Pandya to miss Afghanistan ODIs due to leg sprain
content: Hardik Pandya will miss the upcoming ODI series against Afghanistan due to a mild leg sprain sustained during training. The all-rounder, who was at peak fitness, will remain in Bengaluru for at least two weeks for recovery under the guidance of medical staff. This setback prevents him from joining the squad as planned.

title: Bharathiraja: The Aladdin of Tamil cinema
content: Bharathiraja revolutionized Tamil cinema by bringing the raw beauty of rural Tamil Nadu to the screen with "16 Vayadhiniley." He introduced a new era of filmmaking, focusing on ordinary people and launching the careers of numerous actors and technicians. His distinctive style and nurturing of talent left an indelible mark on the industry.

title: Salesforce lays off staff working on Agentforce AI despite major push
content: Salesforce is reportedly conducting another round of job cuts, impacting employees on its Agentforce AI platform. This follows earlier layoffs and a strategic shift towards AI-powered products, with CEO Marc Benioff even considering rebranding the company as Agentforce. Despite past adoption concerns, Salesforce claims Agentforce has achieved over $1 billion in annualized revenue.

title: Wife clings to hubby’s car after spotting him with another woman in UP; dragged 100 metres
content: A woman was dragged nearly 100 metres on the bonnet of a car in Bareilly after confronting her husband, whom she accused of infidelity. Bystanders intervened to rescue her after she sustained injuries and alleged assault and theft by the other woman. An FIR has been registered against both individuals.

title: Inside India's ODI dressing room: Why Gambhir, Gill must act before 2027 WC
content: India's ODI team begins World Cup preparations amid dressing room dynamics concerns. Shubman Gill's captaincy has seen mixed results, with senior players Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli facing questions about their future. Head coach Gautam Gambhir needs to assert authority in the ODI format to align the team for the 2027 tournament.

title: Layoffs announced at Sam Altman's $2.5 billion startup amid revenue struggles
content: Sam Altman's eyeball-scanning startup, Tools for Humanity, is reportedly cutting jobs as it navigates its next strategic phase. Despite a significant valuation and user sign-ups, the company faces challenges in demonstrating revenue generation and regulatory approval for its "Orb" technology. This news emerges as OpenAI, also led by Altman, files for its IPO.

title: Ex-TMC MLA Sabyasachi Dutta held, pelted with eggs and cow dung outside police station
content: Sabyasachi Dutta, the ex-chairperson of Bidhannagar Municipal Corporation, got nabbed for allegedly demanding a whopping Rs 1 crore from a businessman back in 2018. After his arrest, angry protestors showed up outside the police station, chucking eggs and cow dung at the place. Dutta, however, is sticking to his guns, vehemently denying all the accusations and proclaiming his innocence.

title: Atletico mock Real Madrid after rejecting Alvarez's €150 million bid
content: Real Madrid publicly confirmed a €150 million bid for Julian Alvarez, but city rivals Atletico Madrid rejected the offer and pointed to the striker's release clause of €500 million. Alvarez scored 20 goals and provided nine assists last season. The rejection gained extra attention after Atletico appeared to mock Real on social media with laughing emojis following the announcement.

title: TCS chairman N Chandrasekaran says company has no layoff plans
content: TCS Chairman N Chandrasekaran confirmed no layoffs and a continued hiring strategy, though massive campus hiring may cease. He emphasized AI as a significant opportunity, not a threat, with AI revenues nearing $2.5 billion annually and projected to be 100% of revenue by 2028-2030. AI is seen as intelligence infrastructure, creating new opportunities and talent needs.

title: Watch: Explosion seen at US 5th Fleet headquarters in Bahrain as Iran retaliates
content: Iran's IRGC claimed a drone attack on the US Navy's Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain, alleging it was retaliation for recent US airstrikes on Iranian infrastructure. The IRGC stated the operation targeted the base in Manama after US strikes damaged civilian facilities in Jask, Sirik, and Qeshm, warning of stronger responses to further US military action.

title: House ramps & road encroachments: When can authorities issue notices or order demolition?
content: Civic bodies across India make headlines as they crack down on public space encroachments. Laws like the Control of National Highways Act, 2002, and state-specific regulations empower authorities to remove unauthorised structures. Penalties range from fines to demolition, with Supreme Court directives ensuring due notice before action, safeguarding against arbitrary 'Bulldozer Justice'.

title: Watch: Trump's 'proportional response' to Iran after helicopter attack
content: Following Iran's downing of a US Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz, President Trump ordered retaliatory strikes on Iranian air defense systems. This escalation intensifies months of Middle East tensions, disrupting shipping and raising global energy prices. Iran vowed a "decisive response," casting doubt on ceasefire efforts amid ongoing regional hostilities.

title: Who is Yamini Rangan? Indian-origin engineer who is one of tech's highest-paid CEOs
content: Yamini Rangan, an Indian-origin tech executive, rose from engineering studies in India to become the CEO of HubSpot. With over two decades of experience across various tech roles, she now leads the global software company, focusing on innovation and customer experience. Her journey exemplifies the power of combining technical education with business acumen and leadership.

title: Friends with Kohli, England's FIFA WC-bound captain teases RCB with witty post
content: England captain Harry Kane amused fans by posting, “Always ready if needed, @englandcricket & @RCBTweets!” after playing cricket during FIFA World Cup preparations. A friend of Virat Kohli, Kane recently congratulated IPL champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru. Meanwhile, England boss Thomas Tuchel is expected to manage Kane's workload during the World Cup.

title: Meerut model alleges 10-year exploitation on marriage pretext, conversion pressure
content: A Meerut model has accused a man of exploiting her for a decade, promising marriage while allegedly extorting ₹50 lakh and forcing her to convert religion. She claims he assaulted her and threatened to release private content when she resisted. Police are investigating the complaint filed at the SSP's office.

title: Veteran Tamil filmmaker Bharathiraja passes away at 84
content: Renowned Tamil filmmaker Bharathiraja, celebrated for his realistic portrayal of rural life, has passed away at 84. His groundbreaking films like '16 Vayathinile' redefined Tamil cinema. Remembered by industry colleagues and fans, his immense legacy as a visionary storyteller and mentor will continue to inspire generations.

title: Uttarakhand Dalit teen ‘tortured to death’ over friendship with upper caste girl
content: A Dalit teenager's life was tragically cut short in Tehri Garhwal after allegedly being held captive and brutally assaulted by the family of an upper-caste girl he was friends with. The victim's father claims nails were hammered into his son's legs. Police have arrested the girl's father and grandfather, initiating murder charges.

title: Birthday cake nightmare: Maharashtra 3-year-old swallows 2 hidden metal pins
content: A toddler's third birthday celebration turned into a nightmare when he unknowingly swallowed two metal pins hidden in chocolate decorations on his cake. The pins, used as scaffolding by the bakery, were lodged in his small intestine, causing 48 hours of terror for his family before passing naturally. The incident highlights the dangers of hidden objects in children's food.

title: Court bars husband contact: Delhi judge grants Sapna Choudhary interim relief
content: In a significant legal ruling, a Delhi court has provided interim relief to Haryanvi dance sensation Sapna Choudhary, safeguarding her against her estranged husband's alleged violent behavior. The court has imposed a prohibition on him from making any form of contact, highlighting concerns for her personal safety and the impending release of her new film.

title: 'Sad way to go': Ex-England cricketer breaks silence on Stokes retirement talks
content: England captain Ben Stokes faces scrutiny over a reported nightclub incident involving a rugby player, alongside an ECB investigation for alleged curfew breaches. This controversy fuels speculation about his future in cricket, with retirement reportedly considered. Former player Ravi Bopara urges patience, emphasizing Stokes' importance and hoping any departure isn't solely due to this situation.

title: Suryakumar Yadav: The 'disruptor' who led India's T20 revolution
content: India still had one foot in an older version of T20 cricket when Surya came into the side. Now when he has been left out of the squad, India have become a team filled with T20 specialists who go full throttle from ball one and hit more sixes than ever before. Surya was not solely responsible for that change, but he was at its centre.

title: NEET UG re exam 2026: NTA opens scribe details portal for PwD/PwBD candidates
content: The National Testing Agency has opened the Scribe Details Portal for eligible PwD/PwBD candidates appearing in the NEET UG 2026 re-examination. The portal is active from June 9 to June 12, allowing candidates to submit scribe details for the exam scheduled on June 21. NTA has also released the city intimation slip for candidates to plan their travel and accommodation.

title: £25 rum and cokes and 'tipsy' England captain: New details emerge on Stokes' nightclub incident
content: England cricket captain Ben Stokes and teammate Gus Atkinson were involved in a nightclub altercation in Chelsea after drinking with England rugby players, including Maro Itoje. A dispute over VIP tables allegedly sparked the incident. The ECB is investigating a breach of team protocols, and both players could miss England’s next Test while the inquiry continues.

title: Caitlin Clark opens up about handling constant criticism and scrutiny
content: Caitlin Clark addressed recent criticism surrounding her emotional on-court behavior after leading the Indiana Fever to a dramatic 78-76 win over the Washington Mystics. The Fever star defended the passion that has become a hallmark of her game, saying it helps create a deeper connection with fans. Clark acknowledged there are moments when emotions can be managed better but stressed that her competitive fire is essential to who she is as a player.

title: Hitting 193 km/h at 44, Serena Williams' tennis return steals the show - Watch
content: After nearly four years away, Serena Williams made a winning return to professional tennis at Queen's Club, partnering Victoria Mboko in doubles. The 44-year-old fired serves up to 193 km/h as the pair beat third seeds Nicole Melichar-Martinez and Erin Routliffe 7-6 (2), 6-2. Williams praised Mboko after the victory, while the teenager called it an honour to play alongside the tennis legend.

title: Simone Biles & Jonathan Owens' 2026 net worth and luxury lifestyle
content: Simone Biles and Jonathan Owens have an estimated combined net worth of $27 million in 2026. Simone accounts for about $25 million through endorsements, appearances, tours, and investments, while Jonathan’s $2 million comes mainly from NFL contracts. The couple also owns valuable real estate and enjoys a luxury lifestyle, helping secure their financial future beyond sports.

title: Jason and Kylie Kelce’s 2026 net worth reflects success beyond football
content: Jason Kelce is a Super Bowl-winning former Philadelphia Eagles center. And his wife Kylie Kelce is a former field hockey player. They boast a combined est. net worth of $61 million in 2026. Their wealth stems from Jason’s NFL career earnings, lucrative ESPN contract, and his popular New Heights podcast. Alongside Kylie’s media ventures and brand partnerships with Dove for instance.

title: Blake Lively faces a fresh setback as Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce plan wedding
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have had a lot to deal with in the last few months as they gear up to get married as many remain curious about the pop star’s bond with Blake Lively, sparking concern among many fans. While both Taylor Swift and the popular actress Blake Lively remain publicly distant from one another, it seems Lively is going through a lot in her life.

title: Watch: Ball boy touches Gill's feet after India captain's ton vs Afghanistan
content: India captain Shubman Gill's heartwarming gesture towards a young ball boy after scoring a century went viral following India's dominant innings and 300-run victory over Afghanistan. Debutant Manav Suthar stole the show with seven wickets, marking a dream start to his Test career.

title: Why Pakistan's iconic Gaddafi Stadium has been penalised by the ICC
content: Lahore's Gaddafi Stadium has been handed one demerit point by the ICC for an "unsatisfactory" pitch during the Pakistan-Australia ODI, deemed too slow and spin-friendly. Similarly, Lord's in London received a demerit point for excessive seam movement and variable bounce in the England-New Zealand Test. Both venues now face potential suspensions if further demerit points are accumulated.

title: Bharathiraja passes away | Tributes pour in for Tamil cinema's transformative voice
content: A towering personality in the film industry, Bharathiraja was fondly known as Iyakkunar Imayam (Pinnacle among Directors)

content: The VMC issues the orders after the High Court’s directive; some vendors claim that they are not unauthorised ones, but their vending certificates were not renewed in time

content: A book on the Nicholases, ‘The Keeper of the Wells’, is all set to be launched at the Victoria Public Hall on July 11

content: While the Education Ministry said recruitments were happening across Centrally Funded Technical Institutes, RTI data show 35.2% of posts remain vacant

content: A hand grenade exploded accidentally during the routine handing over of equipment at a camp in Kamalkote in Uri, the officials said

content: A total of 28 trees and saplings will be cut inside the park for the construction of the Chennai Port-Maduravoyal Elevated Corridor; NHAI source says that the park will be restored after the corridor is built, and its functioning will not be affected

content: At around 2.30 a.m., nearly 100 residents laid siege to the EB office in Arumbakkam, demanding immediate restoration of power; similar protests were witnessed in Washermenpet, Tiruvottiyur, Ambattur, Madhavaram, Manali, Avadi, Poonamallee, Thirunindravur, Medavakkam, and Velachery

content: The PM’s visit to France will take place in two segments, with the first segment stretching from June 13 to June 14, and the second taking place from June 16 to June 19; he will hold a meeting with Mr. Macron on June 14

content: ‘Today their leader is Narendra Modi; they have become BJP,’ says Kalyan Banerjee, Chief Whip of AITC in the Lok Sabha, on the rebel Trinamool Congress MPs

title: Vijay to attend NITI Aayog Governing Council meeting in Delhi
content: Sources said Mr. Vijay is scheduled to leave Chennai for New Delhi on a special flight on Wednesday. In New Delhi, he is expected to meet some political leaders.

title: Delhi data centre fire may have destroyed equipment worth hundreds of crores
content: The fire, which broke out in the early morning hours of June 5, has led to major equipment losses for companies hosting servers there, and caused disruptions for many local internet operators.

content: The temple was inaugurated by the previous Trinamool Congress government in 2025; BJP leaders and religious authorities from Puri objected to the temple being called ‘Jagannath Dham’

content: A retired army officer had filed a complaint, accusing traffic policemen of misbehaving with him and his family during a weekend drunk-driving check, police said on Tuesday.

title: PM Modi, in call with Amir, slams attack on Kuwait
content:
.

NEW DELHI: Prime Minister Narendra Modi expressed deep concern over escalation of tensions and reaffirmed India’s strong condemnation of attacks on Kuwait’s sovereignty and territorial integrity in a telephone conversation with the Amir of Kuwait, Sheikh Meshal Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al Sabah. The two leaders exchanged views on the evolving security situation in West Asia, said an Indian readout. PM Modi reiterated the call for de-escalation, dialogue and diplomacy for earliest restoration of peace and stability. “The PM thanked His Highness the Amir for his personal attention to the continued well-being and the safety of the large Indian community in Kuwait,” said the readout. PM Modi spoke to the Amir days after an attack on Kuwait International Airport that left one Indian dead and several others injured. India strongly condemned the attack and urged all to not target civilians and civilian infrastructure.

End of Article

title: Revanth gets a rap from Congress for ‘Hitler inspiration’ remark
content: congress has ticked off CM Revanth Reddy for his remark, calling it out of line with the party ethos and ideology . it has also asked the CM to be careful with his comments in future . congress censure comes after controversy triggered by Reddy’s statement that Hitler was the inspiration behind the constitution of the Hyderabad disaster response and asset protection agency .

title: BJP’s ‘Nabin’ team to take charge of party organisation soon
content: organisational teams are customarily rejigged after a new president is elected . the new office-bearers will be announced in about a week, a source said . BJP brass emphasising the need for the party to be more representative and youthful .

title: Trinamool dares rebels to quit party and join BJP
content: the rebel MPs claim they have the numbers required under the Tenth Schedule . BJP has not officially accepted them despite their efforts to align with it, they said . the rebels have reportedly written to Lok Sabha speaker Om Birla .

title: 12 years in PMO: NDA to celebrate PM Modi overtaking Nehru
content: the conclave will come days after 23 opposition met under the egis of INDIA bloc . the difference in mood and context of the twin gatherings of the country’s principal poles could not have been starker . govt's achievements coupled with Modi’s own milestone likely to dominate proceedings and resolution .

title: Collective thinking, resolve and dream: 12 years of PM Modi government
content: era of 'Sankalp se Siddhi' has witnessed unprecedented growth in india's development, security, economic empowerment and global prestige . the world sees Bharat not merely as a regional power but as one of the greatest powers of the 21st century . in 2014, PM first gave the nation ‘Collective Thinking’, then on the strength of public trust .
